reviews.review_description:
Planet Attack is a very cool indie gem that delivers pure arcade satisfaction in VR. You pilot an upgradeable spaceship — enhancing both the ship itself and its weapon systems — as you take on hostile planets, demolishing enemy structures while dodging defensive fire and flying debris.

I’m proud to say I finished it and enjoyed every single minute! The gameplay feels perfectly suited for VR, with solid mechanics, great pacing, and just the right amount of challenge. I’d love to see new content or expanded missions because this one is simply fun — a great example of how a smaller indie title can deliver big enjoyment.

I love the concept of the game, it reminds me of the good old times of space fighter games and the art style if the game enhances that feeling. The flight and shooting mechanics are intuitive and addictive. The missions are short and intense and you can upgrade your space ship after every mission. The devs made pretty good cutscenes in which the story is introduced and a tutorial does a good job in teaching the general mechanics of the game. All in all a worthwhile VR experience which I recommend to every Retro Sci-Fi Fan out there
Reminds me of the old rogue squadron game but more immersive with VR.

Liked this one very much, especially the shooting mechanic.

Very unique and appealing concept
